description: Experimental gas-phase standard enthalpies of formation (delta_f H, 298.15 K) for small organic molecules within ANI-2x element coverage (H, C, N, O, F, S, Cl). Values curated from NIST Chemistry WebBook (primary, fetched 2026-07-22), corroborated against CODATA Key Values, NIST-JANAF, ATcT, and the Pedley/Cox-Pilcher compilations where available.
caveat: These are experimental enthalpies of FORMATION relative to elemental reference states (C graphite, H2, N2, O2, F2, S rhombic, Cl2 gas). An ML potential (ANI-2x) or ab initio method yields a total electronic (or atomization) energy, NOT delta_f H. To compare like-with-like you must convert: delta_f H(298) = sum over atoms [ delta_f H(atom, gas, 298) ] - D0(molecule) - [H(298)-H(0)]_thermal + ZPE handling. Concretely: (1) atomize the molecule using the SAME method to get the atomization energy at 0 K, (2) add experimental atomic delta_f H (gas-phase, 0 K or 298 K) for each constituent element, (3) apply zero-point vibrational energy (ZPE) and 0->298 K thermal enthalpy corrections consistently. Do NOT compare a raw single-point energy to delta_f H. See README.md 'Enthalpy-of-formation methodology' for the full recipe and pitfalls.

verification_legend.cross-checked: Two or more independent experimental determinations (or CODATA + JANAF) agree within combined uncertainty.
verification_legend.single-source: Only one reliable determination reachable this session, or sources disagree beyond uncertainty (see note).

entries.name: Water

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (CODATA Key Values, Cox, Wagman et al. 1984)

entries.note: CODATA review value; NIST-JANAF (Chase 1998) gives -241.83. Anchor value, effectively exact.

entries.name: Ammonia

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (CODATA Key Values, Cox, Wagman et al. 1984)

entries.note: CODATA review value; NIST-JANAF (Chase 1998) gives -45.90.

entries.name: Methane

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Manion 2002, adopting Gurvich, Veyts et al. 1991)

entries.note: Corroborated by combustion calorimetry (Pittam & Pilcher 1972: -74.5 +/- 0.4). NIST-JANAF older value -74.87. ATcT ~ -74.52.

entries.name: Ethane

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Manion 2002, adopting Gurvich, Veyts et al. 1991)

entries.note: Corroborated by Pittam & Pilcher 1972 (-83.8 +/- 0.3).

entries.name: Propane

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Pittam & Pilcher 1972, combustion calorimetry)

entries.note: Corroborated by Prosen & Rossini 1945 (-103.8 +/- 0.6, from heat of hydrogenation).

entries.name: Ethylene (ethene)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Manion 2002, adopting Gurvich, Veyts et al. 1991)

entries.note: NIST-JANAF (Chase 1998) gives 52.47. Values in close agreement.

entries.name: Propene (propylene)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Furuyama, Golden et al. 1969; Lacher et al. 1950)

entries.note: Two independent methods give 20.41; reanalyzed combustion data (Cox & Pilcher) gives 19.7-19.8.

entries.name: Acetylene (ethyne)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Manion 2002, adopting Gurvich, Veyts et al. 1991)

entries.note: NIST-JANAF gives 226.73; Prosen 226.7. ATcT reports a slightly higher +228.3. Use +227-228 range with care.

entries.name: Benzene

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Roux, Temprado et al. 2008, critical review)

entries.note: Corroborated by Prosen et al. 1945 (82.93 +/- 0.50) and Good & Smith 1969 (82.8). Classic benchmark value.

entries.name: Methanol

source.database: NIST CCCBDB experimental (-201.00 +/- 0.60); Pedley 1994 compilation

entries.note: NIST WebBook lists a less precise 9-value average of -205 +/- 10 and a derived -201.5; the accepted value is -201.0 +/- 0.6, matched by CCCBDB and ATcT (~ -200.9).

entries.name: Ethanol

source.database: Pedley 1994 compilation; NIST Chemistry WebBook 9-value average (-234 +/- 2)

entries.note: NIST WebBook average -234 +/- 2 is consistent with the Pedley recommended -234.8 +/- 0.5.

entries.name: Formaldehyde

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Fletcher & Pilcher 1970, combustion calorimetry)

entries.note: Corroborated by ATcT (~ -109.2). The older NIST-JANAF value -115.90 is superseded; do NOT use it.

entries.name: Acetaldehyde

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Wiberg, Crocker & Morgan 1991, hydrogenation calorimetry)

entries.note: CAUTION: notable inter-source spread. This Wiberg value is -170.7; older Pedley/Cox-Pilcher compilations and ATcT give ~ -166 to -165.5. Re-verify against ATcT before using as a hard bench target.

entries.name: Acetic acid

source.database: Pedley 1994 compilation; NIST Chemistry WebBook 8-value average (-433 +/- 3)

entries.note: NIST WebBook average -433 +/- 3 consistent with Pedley -432.8 +/- 1.5.

entries.name: Formic acid

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Guthrie 1974; Sinke 1959; Lebedeva 1964)

entries.note: Five determinations cluster -378.3 to -379.2; adopted -378.6.

entries.name: Methylamine

source.database: Pedley 1994 compilation; NIST Chemistry WebBook (Aston et al. 1937: -23.5)

entries.note: NIST/Aston -23.5 and Pedley -22.5 agree within ~1 kJ/mol; literature also cites -23.4 +/- 1.0.

entries.name: Hydrogen cyanide

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook / NIST-JANAF (Chase 1998)

entries.note: Classic NIST-JANAF value +135.14 (no uncertainty listed). Some modern evaluations (ATcT) place it a few kJ/mol lower; verify before use as a hard target.

entries.name: Pyridine

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Cox et al. 1954; Andon et al. 1957; Hubbard et al. 1961)

entries.note: Three independent measurements cluster 140.2-140.7 kJ/mol.

entries.name: Furan

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Guthrie, Scott et al. 1952, combustion calorimetry)

entries.note: Adopted calorimetric value -34.7; Pedley gives -34.8. Indirect liquid+vaporization estimates (-27.7, -29.8) are less reliable.

entries.name: Thiophene

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Hubbard, Scott et al. 1955, combustion calorimetry)

entries.note: Direct calorimetry 115.0 +/- 1.0; matched by Pedley. Indirect liquid-based estimates (116-218) unreliable.

entries.name: Chloromethane (methyl chloride)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Fletcher & Pilcher 1971; Manion 2002)

entries.note: Fletcher & Pilcher -81.96 +/- 0.67 and Manion -81.9 +/- 1.5 agree. NIST-JANAF older value -83.68 differs.

entries.name: Fluoromethane (methyl fluoride)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook / NIST-JANAF (Chase 1998)

entries.note: NIST-JANAF review value -234.30 (no uncertainty listed). Only fluorine-containing entry; an ion-based value -247 exists but is less reliable. Verify before hard use.

entries.name: Carbon dioxide

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (CODATA Key Values, Cox, Wagman et al. 1984)

entries.note: CODATA review value; NIST-JANAF -393.52. Anchor value.

entries.name: Carbon monoxide

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (CODATA Key Values, Cox, Wagman et al. 1984)

entries.note: CODATA review value; NIST-JANAF -110.53. Anchor value.

entries.name: Hydrogen sulfide

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (CODATA Key Values, Cox, Wagman et al. 1984)

entries.note: CODATA review value; NIST-JANAF -20.50.

entries.name: Methanethiol (methyl mercaptan)

source.database: NIST Chemistry WebBook (Good, Lacina & McCullough 1961, rotating-bomb calorimetry)

entries.note: Corroborated by Pedley (-22.9 +/- 0.6).
